blocks.content:
## Agent Infrastructure

*The substrate that lets AI actually write code*

Master the Observe-Think-Act-Evaluate loop, tool use and function calling, context management, multi-step reasoning, error recovery, build-fork-integrate decisions, agent memory, multi-agent orchestration, and prompt architecture.

## Modules in This Level

### 4.1 What a Coding Agent Actually Is

The difference is not the model. It is the loop.

### 4.2 Tool Use and Function Calling

Tool use is the mechanism by which a language model crosses the boundary from text to action. Understanding it at the protocol level is not optional for a Platform PM — because every product decision 

### 4.3 Context Management and Retrieval

Context management is the highest-leverage platform engineering problem in agent infrastructure. Model capability matters; context engineering matters more. A weaker model with excellent context wins 

### 4.4 Multi-Step Reasoning and Planning

Planning is the capability that separates a coding agent from a code-writing assistant. The assistant writes the code you ask for. The agent figures out what code to write, in what order, and verifies

### 4.5 Error Recovery and Graceful Degradation

Error recovery is where the difference between a demo and a production system becomes visible. Every agent demo shows the happy path. Production systems fail at step 7 of 12, and the quality of recove

### 4.6 Build, Fork, or Integrate?

The build-vs-buy decision for agent infrastructure is one of the most consequential calls a Platform PM makes. Get it wrong and you have either built something a vendor does better for free, or ceded 

### 4.7 Agentic Workflows in a Design Tool

The canonical coding agent lives in a terminal or an IDE. Its context is text: file contents, shell output, error messages, documentation. Make's agent lives in a design tool. Its context includes a v

### 4.8 Cost, Latency, and Token Economics

Token economics is where agent platform strategy meets business model. A Platform PM who cannot do this math is flying blind on one of the most important constraints in the product.
